title: Saint Bavo Cathedral

infobox.value: ([Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"): *Sint Baafskathedraal*), is a [Roman Catholic](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church") [cathedral](/wiki/Cathedral "Cathedral") in [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent"), Belgium. The 89-metre-tall (292 ft) [Gothic](/wiki/Gothic_architecture "Gothic architecture") building is the seat of the [Diocese of Ghent](/wiki/Diocese_of_Ghent "Diocese of Ghent") and is named for [Saint Bavo of Ghent](/wiki/Bavo_of_Ghent "Bavo of Ghent"). It contains the well-known *[Ghent Altarpiece](/wiki/Ghent_Altarpiece "Ghent Altarpiece")*, also called the *Adoration of the Mystic Lamb*.

Church in Ghent, Belgium

**Saint Bavo's Cathedral**, also known as **Sint-Baafs Cathedral** ([Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"): *Sint Baafskathedraal*), is a [Roman Catholic](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church") [cathedral](/wiki/Cathedral "Cathedral") in [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent"), Belgium. The 89-metre-tall (292 ft) [Gothic](/wiki/Gothic_architecture "Gothic architecture") building is the seat of the [Diocese of Ghent](/wiki/Diocese_of_Ghent "Diocese of Ghent") and is named for [Saint Bavo of Ghent](/wiki/Bavo_of_Ghent "Bavo of Ghent"). It contains the well-known *[Ghent Altarpiece](/wiki/Ghent_Altarpiece "Ghent Altarpiece")*, also called the *Adoration of the Mystic Lamb*.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint_Bavo%27s_Cathedral,_Ghent&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/98/Gent_Baafs_009.JPG/250px-Gent_Baafs_009.JPG)](/wiki/File:Gent_Baafs_009.JPG)

Cathedral from the Maaseikplein

The cathedral stands on the site of the former Chapel of St. John the Baptist, which was primarily of wooden construction and was consecrated in 942 by Transmarus, [Bishop of Tournai and Noyon](/wiki/Bishop_of_Tournai_and_Noyon "Bishop of Tournai and Noyon"). Traces of a later [Romanesque](/wiki/Romanesque_architecture "Romanesque architecture") structure can be found in the cathedral's crypt.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Construction of the [Gothic](/wiki/Gothic_architecture "Gothic architecture") church began around 1274.

In the subsequent period from the 14th through 16th centuries, nearly continuous expansion projects in the Gothic style were executed on the structure. A new [choir](/wiki/Choir_(architecture) "Choir (architecture)"), [radiating chapels](/wiki/Apse_chapel "Apse chapel"), expansions of the [transepts](/wiki/Transept "Transept"), a [chapter house](/wiki/Chapter_house "Chapter house"), [nave](/wiki/Nave "Nave") aisles and a single-tower western section were all added.

In 1539, as a result of the rebellion against [Charles V](/wiki/Charles_V,_Holy_Roman_Emperor "Charles V, Holy Roman Emperor"), who was baptized in the church, the old Abbey of St. Bavo was dissolved. Its abbot and monks went on to become canons in a chapter that was attached to what then became the Church of St. Bavo. When the [Diocese of Ghent](/wiki/Diocese_of_Ghent "Diocese of Ghent") was founded in 1559, the church became its [cathedral](/wiki/Cathedral "Cathedral"). Construction was considered complete on 7 June 1569.

In the summer of 1566, bands of [Calvinist](/wiki/Calvinism "Calvinism") [iconoclasts](/wiki/Iconoclasm "Iconoclasm") visited Catholic churches in the Netherlands, including St. Bavo, shattering stained-glass windows, smashing statues, and destroying paintings and other artworks they perceived as idolatrous.[[2]](#cite_note-2) However, the altarpiece by the Van Eycks was saved.

## Interior

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint_Bavo%27s_Cathedral,_Ghent&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Interior")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/db/Interieur_-_Sint-Baafskathedraal_Gent_%283%29.jpg/250px-Interieur_-_Sint-Baafskathedraal_Gent_%283%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Interieur_-_Sint-Baafskathedraal_Gent_(3).jpg)

Interior

### Choir

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint_Bavo%27s_Cathedral,_Ghent&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Choir")]

The most impressive part is the high choir with stalls for the members of the Chapter of Saint-Bavon. The episcopal throne is located on the right side with the episcopal arms visible. Highlights of the interior decoration of the choir include the [Baroque](/wiki/Baroque "Baroque") high altar (1702–1782), in white, black, and red flamed marble, and the tomb monuments of Ghent bishops, including that of Antonius Triest, in white and black marble (1652–1654), a major work of [Jerôme Duquesnoy (II)](/wiki/Jer%C3%B4me_Duquesnoy_(II) "Jerôme Duquesnoy (II)"). On the right side is the gallery with painted crests of the members of the [Order of the Golden Fleece](/wiki/Order_of_the_Golden_Fleece "Order of the Golden Fleece").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/da/Altar_of_Saint_Bavo%27s_Cathedral%2C_Ghent%2C_Belgium.jpg/250px-Altar_of_Saint_Bavo%27s_Cathedral%2C_Ghent%2C_Belgium.jpg)](/wiki/File:Altar_of_Saint_Bavo%27s_Cathedral,_Ghent,_Belgium.jpg)

Baroque high altar

In the choir is the 1559 painting *[The Queen of Sheba visits King Solomon](/wiki/The_Queen_of_Sheba_visits_King_Solomon "The Queen of Sheba visits King Solomon")* by the Flemish artist [Lucas de Heere](/wiki/Lucas_de_Heere "Lucas de Heere"). This allegorical work depicts [King Solomon](/wiki/King_Solomon "King Solomon") as [Philip II of Spain](/wiki/Philip_II_of_Spain "Philip II of Spain"), recognizable by his facial features, receiving gifts from the [Queen of Sheba](/wiki/Queen_of_Sheba "Queen of Sheba"), an allegory of the [Low Countries](/wiki/Low_Countries "Low Countries"), representing that country donating its riches to the Spanish king in thanks for his prudent government.

### Nave

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint_Bavo%27s_Cathedral,_Ghent&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Nave")]

[![Pulpit by Laurent Delvaux in St. Bavo's Cathedral](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/6a/Preekstoel_-_Laurent_Delvaux_-_Sint-Baafskathedraal_Gent_%2825%29.jpg/250px-Preekstoel_-_Laurent_Delvaux_-_Sint-Baafskathedraal_Gent_%2825%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Preekstoel_-_Laurent_Delvaux_-_Sint-Baafskathedraal_Gent_(25).jpg)

Pulpit by Laurent Delvaux

In the nave there is an impressive [Rococo](/wiki/Rococo "Rococo") pulpit (1741–1745), made in oak, gilded wood and white and black marble by [Laurent Delvaux](/wiki/Laurent_Delvaux "Laurent Delvaux"), with [wrought-iron](/wiki/Wrought-iron "Wrought-iron") [railings](/wiki/Guard_rail "Guard rail") by J. Arens. There are also chairs designed by the contemporary designer Maarten Van Severen. The main altar is placed between the nave and the choir.

### Treasury and crypt

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint_Bavo%27s_Cathedral,_Ghent&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Treasury and crypt")]

In the Chapel of the Holiest, an important *[Calvary](/wiki/Calvary "Calvary") Triptych* is on display. This 15th-century work is attributed to [Justus van Gent](/wiki/Justus_van_Gent "Justus van Gent"). Finally, there is a valuable collection of important liturgical plates, reliquaries, and liturgical vessels dating from the 15th century onward. Among the important reliquaries are the head of Saint John the Baptist and of Saint Macarius. The important collection of hand-embroidered and brocaded liturgical ornaments is widely known as one of the most important of the country, some of which are put on display.

* [![Interior of the Romanesque crypt, with frescos of religious figures visible](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/67/St._Bavos_Cathedral_Ghent.jpg/250px-St._Bavos_Cathedral_Ghent.jpg)](/wiki/File:St._Bavos_Cathedral_Ghent.jpg "Interior of the Romanesque crypt, with frescos of religious figures visible")

## Organs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint_Bavo%27s_Cathedral,_Ghent&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Organs")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/77/Sint-Baafskathedraal_Gand_304_orgue.jpg/250px-Sint-Baafskathedraal_Gand_304_orgue.jpg)](/wiki/File:Sint-Baafskathedraal_Gand_304_orgue.jpg)

The great organ built in 1935 by [Klais](/wiki/Klais_Orgelbau "Klais Orgelbau") is the biggest of the [Low Countries](/wiki/Low_Countries "Low Countries")

The cathedral has four organs for use at liturgical celebrations.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Most famous is the main organ in the upper church, the biggest organ in the [Low Countries](/wiki/Low_Countries "Low Countries"). In 1935, Mgr Coppieters commanded that the Klais organ from the world exhibition would be put inside the cathedral. The organ case dates from the 18th century and the complete organ has more than 6,000 pipes inside. It has five manuals.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Art

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint_Bavo%27s_Cathedral,_Ghent&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Art")]

### *Ghent Altarpiece*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint_Bavo%27s_Cathedral,_Ghent&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Ghent Altarpiece")]

Main article: [Ghent Altarpiece](/wiki/Ghent_Altarpiece "Ghent Altarpiece")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8c/Lamgods_open.jpg/330px-Lamgods_open.jpg)](/wiki/File:Lamgods_open.jpg)

The twelve interior panels. This open view measures 5.2 m × 3.75 m (17.1 ft × 12.3 ft).[[5]](#cite_note-5)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d4/Lamgods_closed.jpg/250px-Lamgods_closed.jpg)](/wiki/File:Lamgods_closed.jpg)

Closed view, back panels

The cathedral is noted for the *[Ghent Altarpiece](/wiki/Ghent_Altarpiece "Ghent Altarpiece")*, originally in the Joost Vijd Chapel. It is formally known as the *[Adoration of the Mystic Lamb](/wiki/Ghent_Altarpiece "Ghent Altarpiece")* after its lower centre panel by [Hubert](/wiki/Hubert_van_Eyck "Hubert van Eyck") and [Jan van Eyck](/wiki/Jan_van_Eyck "Jan van Eyck"). This work is considered Van Eyck's masterpiece and one of the most important works of the early [Northern Renaissance](/wiki/Northern_Renaissance "Northern Renaissance"), as well as one of the greatest artistic masterpieces of Belgium.[[6]](#cite_note-nazi_contraband-6) Part of the painting, the lowermost left panel known as *[The Just Judges](/wiki/The_Just_Judges "The Just Judges")*, was stolen in 1934 and has not been recovered. It has since been replaced with a facsimile by [Jef Van der Veken](/wiki/Jef_Van_der_Veken "Jef Van der Veken").

### Other religious art

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint_Bavo%27s_Cathedral,_Ghent&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Other religious art")]

The cathedral is home to the works of other artists of note. It holds the painting *Saint Bavo enters the Convent at Ghent* by [Peter Paul Rubens](/wiki/Peter_Paul_Rubens "Peter Paul Rubens"). There are also works by or after [Lucas de Heere](/wiki/Lucas_de_Heere "Lucas de Heere"), one of which is a *View of Gent*. [Frans Pourbus the Elder](/wiki/Frans_Pourbus_the_Elder "Frans Pourbus the Elder") painted 14 panels representing the *History of Saint Andrew* (1572) and a *Triptych of [Viglius Aytta](/wiki/Viglius "Viglius")* (1571). [Caspar de Crayer](/wiki/Caspar_de_Crayer "Caspar de Crayer") is represented by paintings of *St Macarius of Gent*, *The Beheading of Saint John the Baptist* and *The Martyrdom of Saint Barbara*. The church also holds works by [Antoon van den Heuvel](/wiki/Antoon_van_den_Heuvel "Antoon van den Heuvel") including the *Christ and the Adulterous Woman* and the *Resurrection of Christ*. There are also works by [Lucas van Uden](/wiki/Lucas_van_Uden "Lucas van Uden") and [Jan van Cleef](/wiki/Jan_Van_Cleef "Jan Van Cleef").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

Local Ghent painter [Petrus Norbertus van Reysschoot](/wiki/Petrus_Norbertus_van_Reysschoot "Petrus Norbertus van Reysschoot") painted a series of eleven [grisailles](/wiki/Grisaille "Grisaille"), which decorate the choir of the cathedral, above the stalls. Five of these panels represent scenes from the Old Testament while the other six episodes from the New Testament. These paintings were placed in the cathedral between 1789 and 1791.[[8]](#cite_note-bnb-8)

## Chapter of Saint-Bavo

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint_Bavo%27s_Cathedral,_Ghent&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Chapter of Saint-Bavo")]

Still today the chapter is housed inside the cathedral, since its foundation. Members have been important prelates and members of noble houses. Today [Jozef De Kesel](/wiki/Jozef_De_Kesel "Jozef De Kesel") is one of the honorary canons. The chapters of Haarlem and Ghent are united in friendship. Weekly the canons sing Mass with the bishop.

* [Jacques Ignace van Parys](/w/index.php?title=Jacques_Ignace_van_Parys&action=edit&redlink=1 "Jacques Ignace van Parys (page does not exist)"), died 1702: grandson of Rubens.
* [Constant van Crombrugghe](/wiki/Constant_van_Crombrugghe "Constant van Crombrugghe")
* François III Maria Rubens, died 1720: great-grandson of Rubens.
* [Petrus Joseph Triest](/wiki/Petrus_Joseph_Triest "Petrus Joseph Triest"), founder of the [Brothers of Charity](/wiki/Brothers_of_Charity "Brothers of Charity")
* [Thomas-Philip d'Alsace](/wiki/Thomas_Philip_Wallrad_de_H%C3%A9nin-Li%C3%A9tard_d%27Alsace "Thomas Philip Wallrad de Hénin-Liétard d'Alsace"), Cardinal
